content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You may change any number of its elements (possibly zero), obtaining a new sequence of positive integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​. Each element of BBB must be an integer between 222 and 505050 (both inclusive).

Let's define an undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). For each pair of different vertices iii and jjj, there is an edge between iii and jjj if and only if BiB_iBi​ and BjB_jBj​ are coprime.

You should choose the sequence BBB in such a way that GGG is a connected graph. The number of elements which need to be changed to obtain BBB from AAA should be minimum possible. Find one such sequence BBB and the minimum required number of changes.

It can be proven that a solution always exists — it is always possible to modify sequence AAA in such a way that GGG is connected.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print two lines. The first line should contain a single integer — the minimum required number of changes. The second line should contain N space-separated integers B1, B2, …, BN — the modified sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the graph is already connected, so no changes are needed. For the second test case, we need to change the second element to 3 to make the graph connected.
We need to change the second and third elements to 3 and 5 respectively to make the graph connected.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the minimum number of changes required to make the graph connected. We can start by checking if the graph is already connected. If it is, we don't need to make any changes. If it's not, we need to find the minimum number of changes required to connect the graph. We can use a greedy approach to achieve this. We can start by changing the first element to a prime number, and then check if the graph is connected. If it's not, we can change the next element to a prime number and so on. We can use a helper function to check if two numbers are coprime.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N^2) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of elements in the sequence. The reason for this time complexity is that we are iterating over each test case, and for each test case, we are iterating over each element in the sequence to check if the graph is connected.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of elements in the sequence. The reason for this space complexity is that we need to store the modified sequence.
